operation: First, a 2D sketch is created on the XZ workplane at the origin (0,0,0). The sketch starts at coordinate (2.34, 3.9), draws a vertical line down to Z=0, adds a tangent arc spanning to point (-2.34, 0), draws a vertical line back up to Z=3.9, then closes the profile. This closed sketch is extruded 5.88 units along the Y-axis to form a 3D solid, and the entire final solid is rotated 180 degrees around the Z-axis with the pivot at the origin.
geometry: This is a uniform prismatic solid with a consistent cross-section across its full depth. Its cross-section has a straight horizontal upper edge, two parallel vertical straight edges extending downward from either end of the upper edge, and a smooth convex curved edge connecting the lower ends of the vertical edges. The solid features a flat rectangular top face, two flat rectangular vertical side faces, two matching flat profile end faces, and a continuous convex curved bottom face.
function: This rigid solid component is designed for use as a protective shield insert, load-bearing contact pad, or structural spacer, with a flat upper surface for mounting and a smooth curved lower surface for even load distribution or impact resistance.
